title: An Account of the Battle and Capture of al-$u'luk
description: Following Ibn Laythawayh's defeat of 'Ali b. Aban with the Bahiliyyin in which he had been wounded, `Ali reportedly reached alAhwaz but, without remaining there, made his way to the camp522 of his master, the Zanj leader. There he was treated for his wounds until he completely recovered. `Ali then set out again for al-Ahwaz, despatching at the same time his brother al-Khalil b. Aban and his nephew Abu Sahl Muhammad b. $alih with a huge army against Ibn Laythawayh, who at the time was stationed in cAskar Mukram.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 36
